articles.title: NASA Artemis II Human Research Data Methodology Challenge
articles.description: NASA’s Human Research Program (HRP) uses research to develop methods to protect the health and performance of astronauts in space. articles.title: Artemis 2 astronauts praise performance of Orion
articles.description: The astronauts who flew around the moon on Artemis 2 said they were confident the Orion spacecraft is ready to support future missions. The post Artemis 2 astronauts praise performance of Orion appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: NASA’s Mobile Launcher Arrives at Vehicle Assembly Building
articles.description: After successfully being used to launch the Artemis II lunar test flight on April 1, NASA’s mobile launcher now is inside NASA Kennedy’s Vehicle Assembly Building (VAB) in Florida in preparation for the Artemis III test flight mission rocket stacking operations. articles.title: Artemis 2's heat shield seems to have aced its trial by fire
articles.description: The heat shield on Artemis 2's Orion capsule appears to have held up incredibly well during its scorching downward trip through Earth's atmosphere, mission commander Reid Wiseman said.

articles.title: Landing astronauts on the moon 'is absolutely doable, and it's doable soon,' NASA's Artemis 2 commander says
articles.description: The Artemis 2 astronauts said they gained a deeper understanding of their Orion capsule during their moon mission —and what they learned bodes well for future Artemis flights.

articles.title: 'That was a wonderful toilet': Artemis 2 commander defends mission's lunar loo
articles.description: Artemis 2's groundbreaking space toilet doesn't deserve the bad press it's gotten over the past two weeks, according to mission commander Reid Wiseman.

articles.title: All Points signs agreement to build payload processing facilities at KSC
articles.description: All Points Logistics has signed an agreement with NASA’s Kennedy Space Center to construct satellite processing facilities on center property. The post All Points signs agreement to build payload processing facilities at KSC appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: NASA Invites Media to Latvia Artemis Accords Signing Ceremony
articles.description: The Republic of Latvia will sign the Artemis Accords during a ceremony at 9 a.m. EDT Monday, April 20, at NASA Headquarters in Washington. articles.title: How space weather forecasting keeps astronauts (and satellites) safe
articles.description: BOULDER, Colorado – The sun’s volatile outbursts, such as storms, flares and other space weather, can cause serious harm to astronauts like the Artemis 2 crew who recently came home, and to satellites. That’s why the Space Weather Prediction Center (SWPC) in Boulder, Colorado — part of the National

articles.title: At the Edge of Light
articles.description: In this photo taken on April 6, 2026, a portion of the Moon’s far side is seen along the terminator—the boundary between lunar day and night—where low-angle sunlight casts long shadows across the surface. articles.title: Artemis 2 is a benchmark in our space exploration program — and it depends on steady NASA funding
articles.description: Now that the Artemis 2 mission has been successfully completed, it’s worth taking a look at where NASA stands on the role of humans in exploring space and what its path forward should be. articles.title: NASA’s Mobile Launcher Rolls Ahead of Artemis III Preparation
articles.description: Following the conclusion of NASA’s Artemis II test flight, teams at the agency’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida are shifting focus to Artemis III, which is targeted to launch next year, by rolling the mobile launcher from Launch Complex 39B to NASA’s Kennedy Vehicle Assembly Building (VAB) in Flori

articles.title: Max Space unveils new expandable space habitat for the moon and beyond: 'We need real estate that is scalable'
articles.description: "They are truly ready for long duration space flights and lunar habitation."

articles.title: A new Earthrise: An Apollo historian experiences Artemis 2
articles.description: As Artemis 2 approached, my anticipation was mixed with uncertainty. Would this new Moon mission spark the wonder and excitement I'd felt during Apollo? Those doubts didn't last long.

articles.title: I Am Artemis: Rebekah Tolatovicz
articles.description: Listen to this audio excerpt from Rebekah Tolatovicz, a mechanical technician lead supporting the Orion spacecraft’s main contractor Lockheed Martin: At NASA’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida, there is a fleet of Orion spacecraft in work, and Rebekah Tolatovicz’s hands have helped build each one. articles.title: Go behind the scenes of NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission with NOVA's 'Return to the Moon' documentary tonight (interview)
articles.description: The new Artemis 2 NOVA documentary "Return to the Moon" will air on PBS Wednesday (April 15) to offer a behind-the-scenes look at the moon mission.

articles.title: NASA's Artemis 2 moonshot was just the 'opening act' for America's return to the moon, space agency chief says
articles.description: Artemis 2 kicks off a lunar "relay race" that will lead the NASA and its international partners to a moon base and, eventually, Mars, NASA chief Jared Isaacman says.

articles.title: NASA's Artemis 2 moon shot just landed on Saturday Night Live — space toilet jokes and all (video)
articles.description: NASA's Artemis 2 moon mission wowed the world enough to earn a space spoof skit on Saturday Night Live one day after a perfect splashdown. Watch how it went.

articles.title: This Artemis 2 astronaut really loves Rise | Space photo of the day for April 14, 2026
articles.description: "Rise Wiseman" might be the most beloved mission mascot in history.

articles.title: Artemis 2 calling ISS! Watch the farthest-ever astronaut call from the moon to Earth (video)
articles.description: Astronauts aboard Artemis 2 and the ISS connected across a record-breaking Earth–moon distance in the farthest crew call ever made in space.

articles.title: How Artemis 2 commander Reid Wiseman saved the mission's moon mascot: 'It's hard not to love this little guy. I can't let Rise out of my sight'
articles.description: Rise joined the Artemis 2 crew as a mascot of the moon, bearing 5.6 million names. But the mascot quickly took on a new meaning.

articles.title: Artemis 2 astronauts celebrate successful return to Earth | Space photo of the day for April 13, 2026
articles.description: Artemis 2's Victor Glover and Christina Koch are all smiles after splashdown.

articles.title: Artemis 2: Our favorite photos from NASA's historic moon mission
articles.description: The Artemis 2 mission to the moon beamed back some incredible photos, and we've rounded up the best ones.

articles.title: 'The most special thing that will ever happen in my life': Artemis 2 astronauts describe their epic moon mission
articles.description: The Artemis 2 astronauts are back on Earth, and they've begun processing their historic moon mission. But it's still tough for them to put the experience into words.

articles.title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 205 — All About Artemis
articles.description: On Episode 205 of This Week In Space, Rod Pyle and Tariq Malik discuss the flight of Artemis 2.

articles.description: Live re-entry updates for NASA’s Artemis II mission will be published on this page. All times are Eastern. 4:27 a.m. The Orion spacecraft is secured in the well deck of USS John P. articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 10: Crew Completes Final Burn Before Splashdown
articles.description: At 2:53 p.m. EDT, the Orion spacecraft ignited its thrusters for 8 seconds, producing a change in velocity of 4.2 feet-per-second and pushing Artemis II toward Earth. articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 10: Crew Sets for Final Burn, Splashdown
articles.description: The Artemis II crew — NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch and CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Jeremy Hansen — began the final phase of their journey home to the songs “Run to the Water” by Live, selected by the crew, and “Free” by Zac Brown Band, as they prepared for th

articles.title: Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon
articles.description: After 10 days, over 800,000 km traveled, and a historic flyby of the Moon, Artemis… The post Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon appeared first on NASASpaceFlight.com.

articles.title: Artemis II closing in on Earth return
articles.description: After a groundbreaking 10-day mission that carried humans farther from Earth than ever before, the… The post Artemis II closing in on Earth return appeared first on NASASpaceFlight.com.

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 9: Second Return Correction Burn Complete
articles.description: At 10:53 p.m. EDT, the Orion spacecraft ignited its thrusters for 9 seconds, producing an acceleration in velocity of 5.3 feet-per-second and pushing the Artemis II crew toward Earth. The crew is now more than halfway home.

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 9: Crew Prepares to Come Home
articles.description: On their last full day in space, the Artemis II crew began the morning with “Lonesome Drifter” by Charley Crockett as they approached Earth at 147,337 miles.

articles.title: Artemis II Flight Day 8: Crew Conducts Key Tests on Return to Earth
